entries.question: Where is the crew badge and credential pickup?
entries.answer: Crew credential pickup is at the Staff Check-In desk, Side Entrance Level 1 (not the main public registration). Open from 6:30am. Bring your confirmation email.

entries.question: Where is the crew entrance?
entries.answer: Crew entrance is the South Side entrance off Loading Bay Road. Use your crew badge to access. Do not use the main public entrance — it slows badge verification at peak attendee arrival.

entries.question: Where is the crew rest area?
entries.answer: Crew rest area is on Level 2, Room 205 (marked 'Staff Only'). Quiet space with seating, lockers, and phone charging. Separate from the public areas.

entries.question: What time does the keynote green room open?
entries.answer: Green room (Speaker Ready Room 202, Level 2) opens at 7:00am both days. AV tech on-site from 7:30am. Speakers should arrive at least 45 minutes before their session.

entries.question: Where is the ops channel and how do I join?
entries.answer: Ops channel is #event-ops in the crew Slack workspace. You should already be in it. If not, ask your team lead — do not post in the public attendee Slack.

entries.question: Where is crew catering and what are the meal times?
entries.answer: Crew catering is in the Staff Canteen, Level B1. Breakfast 7:00–9:00am, Lunch 12:00–2:00pm, Dinner 6:00–8:00pm. Show your crew badge. Dietary requests submitted in advance are tagged on your record.

entries.question: Where can crew park?
entries.answer: Crew parking is in Lot C (East side, off Service Road). Use the crew parking pass on your dashboard. Public Lot A/B are for attendees only — you will be towed after 9am.

entries.question: How do I request additional AV equipment?
entries.answer: Submit an AV request via the ops channel with room name, equipment type, and time needed. Tag @av-ops. Requests within 30 minutes of session start are handled as best-effort only.

entries.question: Where is the first-aid station?
entries.answer: First-aid station is on Level 1, Room 104 (next to the nursing room). A trained first-aider is on-site all day. For emergencies use the ops channel or call the direct safety line: ext. 999.

entries.question: What is the radio channel assignment for crew?
entries.answer: Channel 1: Room Ops / Captains. Channel 2: AV Technicians. Channel 3: Vendor / Logistics. Channel 4: Safety Lead (monitor only). All-crew announcements on Channel 1.

entries.question: Where is the loading dock?
entries.answer: Loading Dock is on the North side of the building, off Industrial Road. Dock A for AV/equipment, Dock B for catering, Dock C for furniture/misc. Gate code: 7743.

entries.question: What is the escalation path for a safety incident?
entries.answer: 1) Report immediately to the ops channel. 2) Call the safety lead direct line: ext. 911. 3) Do NOT attempt to handle a safety incident alone. The agent will page the safety lead automatically when safety keywords are detected.

entries.question: What is the crew Wi-Fi password?
entries.answer: Crew Wi-Fi SSID: 'Staff-Net'. Password is printed on the back of your badge. Do NOT share with attendees — attendees use 'Guest-Event-Wifi' (no password, captive portal).

entries.question: Where do attendees pick up their badges?
entries.answer: Attendee registration is the main Public Entrance, Level 1 (not the crew Side Entrance). Open 8:00am both days. Kiosks are self-service; staffed help desk next to the coffee station.

entries.question: Where is lost and found?
entries.answer: Lost and found is at the Staff Check-In desk, Side Entrance Level 1. Items held for 30 days. Electronics and ID documents are logged separately — ask the desk lead.

entries.question: Where is the lactation / nursing room?
entries.answer: Lactation room is Level 1, Room 107 (next to first-aid). Private, lockable, with fridge and sink. Available to attendees and crew — no booking needed. Cleaning between uses by request via ops channel.

entries.question: Are sessions being recorded?
entries.answer: Keynotes and main-hall sessions are recorded and published post-event. Workshops and breakout rooms are NOT recorded. Speakers must consent — check the session's schedule entry for a 'recording' flag before confirming to a speaker.

entries.question: Where are the accessible and all-gender restrooms?
entries.answer: Accessible restrooms: Level 1 near Hall A, Level 2 near Room 205. All-gender restrooms: Level 1 near the main entrance, Level B1 near the canteen. Maps posted at every stairwell.

entries.question: Where is the nearest ATM or cash machine?
entries.answer: Nearest ATM is in the Level 1 lobby by the main public entrance. There is NO ATM on the crew side — all crew purchases in the Staff Canteen are cashless (tap card or phone).

entries.question: Where are the water refill stations?
entries.answer: Water refill stations: Level 1 by Hall A entrance, Level 2 corridor outside Room 205, Level B1 canteen. Reusable bottles were distributed at crew check-in. Single-use cups available at catering only.

entries.question: What time does teardown start?
entries.answer: Day 2 teardown begins 30 minutes after the closing keynote ends (approx. 5:30pm). Crew should not leave earlier than their team lead releases them. Final sign-out at the Staff Check-In desk — timesheet is pre-filled.

entries.question: Is there translator or interpreter support?
entries.answer: Live interpreters (EN/ES/FR) are booked only for the Day 1 keynote. For any other session, attendees can use the Event App's real-time caption feature. Crew needing language support should flag it in #event-ops and tag @translations.
